page: Microsoft Azure E48ns_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Env6 family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

This instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It does not include local storage, relying instead on remote Premium Storage capabilities. As a network-optimized instance, it is designed to handle high-throughput network traffic. The hardware profile lacks a dedicated GPU, relying entirely on the CPU for computational tasks.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in memory bandwidth and data processing. The server achieved a strong rating in large-block memory read bandwidth at 222.79 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 94.78 GB/sec. It also demonstrated strong performance in Passmark CPU tests, including encryption and database operations, alongside high static web serving throughput of 73.77 GB/sec. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng fell within the average range. LLM inference benchmarks for small and medium models showed strong prompt processing and text generation speeds, while large model inference remained average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E48ns_v6 offers a dense memory allocation relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of local storage maintenance. The lack of bundled local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring external storage attachment. This server is suited for memory-intensive databases, enterprise applications, high-throughput web serving, and memory-bound data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E48ns_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Env6 family, powered by a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio, and includes a 260 MB L3 cache with support for AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, this network-optimized instance does not include local storage, relying on Premium Storage compatibility. Benchmarks show strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth, database operations, and static web serving throughput, alongside average CPU stress-test results. This server is suited for memory-intensive databases, enterprise applications, and high-throughput web serving workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48ns_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, designed for high-throughput database and enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized, network-enhanced virtual machine featuring dedicated high-frequency processors and advanced matrix extensions for demanding database and enterprise workloads.

Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix math
Network-optimized architecture with Premium Storage capability
Strong performance in memory bandwidth and database operations
Suited for memory-intensive databases and enterprise applications
